chunk_summary: Guidance on essential fatty acid (EFA) targets for children, including omega-6 (LA) and omega-3 (ALA) intake, vegetarian DHA sources, and supplementation strategies for neurodevelopmental risk or fat malabsorption.
learning_objectives: Understand age-specific Adequate Intake (AI) values for omega-6 (LA) and omega-3 (ALA) in children.
Identify vegetarian sources of DHA and strategies for supplementation in low-intake scenarios.
Recognize the role of EFAs in growth, neural development, and long-chain fat synthesis.
Learn practical dietary recommendations for Indian diets to meet EFA needs.
key_facts: Adequate Intake (AI) for ages 1–3 years: LA ~7 g/day, ALA ~0.7 g/day; for ages 4–8 years: LA ~10 g/day, ALA ~0.9 g/day.
Vegetarian DHA sources include algae-based supplements; supplementation is advised for children at neurodevelopmental risk or with very low intake.
Seed oils (sunflower, safflower, soybean, groundnut) are rich in LA; mustard oil, soybean oil, flaxseed, chia, and walnuts are rich in ALA.
Mixed oils and whole food sources are preferred over reliance on a single oil.
In fat malabsorption or cholestasis, omega-3 supplementation should be specialist-led due to altered fat handling.
reference_quote: DRI Adequate Intake anchors are practical for teaching: ages 1–3 years LA about 7 g/day and ALA about 0.7 g/day; ages 4–8 years LA about 10 g/day and ALA about 0.9 g/day.
document_key_topics: Essential fatty acid (EFA) intake targets for children
